name: D2R Loot Filter — Intense
description: Hides trash drops such as potions, ammo, Large Charms, and low-priority equipment bases including weak shields; converts inherited black labels to dots; and can crunch gems into compact tier labels such as 1Topaz and PTopaz. Works standalone or stacked after another D2RMM loot filter. Display strings only — drops are unchanged.
author: voc0der

children.name: 100% Rejuv Only
children.description: Hide every potion that is not a Full Rejuvenation Potion: small rejuvs, all healing potions, all mana potions, and thawing/antidote/stamina potions.

children.name: Hide Large Charms
children.description: Hide the Large Charm base label. WARNING: this also hides an unidentified Hellfire Torch drop because it shares the same cm2 base string. Once identified, the Torch's separate name remains visible.

children.name: Hide Throwing Potions
children.description: Hide all gas and oil throwing potions.

children.name: Hide Unpopular Bases
children.description: Aggressively hide 78 low-priority endgame bases, including normal/exceptional Act 2 merc weapons, weak elite shields, and lower Paladin shields. Sacred Targe, Sacred Rondache, Vortex Shield, Gilded Shield/Herald of Zakarum, Monarch, Hyperion, and Troll Nest stay visible. ENDGAME WARNING: hides ALL rarities, including useful leveling bases and their unique/set drops.

children.name: Black Labels to Dots
children.description: Replace labels whose final active inline color is D2's #000/black code (ÿc6) from an earlier D2RMM loot filter with the selected tiny dot. Keep this mod later in load order.

children.name: Gem Crunch
children.description: Rename every gem to a compact colored tier label: 1 = Chipped, 2 = Flawed, 3 = regular, 4 = Flawless, P = Perfect (for example, 1Topaz and PTopaz). Keeps an existing inline gem color when stacked after another filter. See the README for the shared-affix caveat.

children.name: Hidden Item Style
children.description: Hidden items are renamed to a tiny colored dot instead of an empty string, because an empty name renders as an ugly empty highlight box.
